message:
refactor(scanf): attach ScanfRoute to ScanSpec and add invalid_scanf_route sentinel

crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/scanf.rs reorganises the scanf
directive parser so the resolved ScanfRoute lives with the ScanSpec
instead of being re-looked-up at dispatch time:

- ScanSpec grows a `route: ScanfRoute` field so every parsed conversion
  specifier carries its dispatch target, the length-mask compatibility
  word, the leading-whitespace-skip flag, and the argument-category
  tag alongside flag/width/length/conversion/scanset. Consumers of
  ScanSpec can now make the dispatch decision once during parse and
  avoid chasing the SCANF_TABLE entry from the conversion byte at
  each runtime call.

- parse_scanf_format initialises the new field with a default
  `invalid_scanf_route()` at spec-allocation time. That sentinel is a
  new const-fn that returns a ScanfRoute whose handler is
  ScanfHandler::Invalid, length_mask is 0, skips_leading_whitespace is
  false, and arg_category is ScanfArgCategory::None — the "no matching
  table entry" shape. The parser then overwrites the field with the
  real SCANF_TABLE[conversion] entry once the conversion byte is
  known and its length-mask compatibility is validated via
  `scanf_length_allowed`. If length-mask validation fails, the spec
  keeps the invalid sentinel and the parse loop breaks out of the
  directive — consistent with the existing "unsupported / rejected
  directive terminates parse" contract.

.beads/issues.jsonl: a single-line refresh on bd-9hq64 reflecting the
paired br status touch.

No functional change to the scanner's input-matching behaviour — the
dispatch table lookup just moves from call-time to parse-time and the
invalid path is represented explicitly rather than by a separate
"no route found" branch at dispatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
